items.headline: Condo management cannot ban owners from renting out their units, BCA rules
items.summary: The Building and Construction Authority has confirmed that condo by-laws cannot override an owner's legal right to rent out their unit, even if the management corporation has passed rules trying to restrict it. The ruling clarifies a long-running point of confusion between condo committees and individual owners.
items.meaning: If you own a condo, no management committee can legally stop you from renting it out, no matter what the estate's house rules say.

items.headline: Condo resale prices rebounded in May 2026 even as fewer units were sold
items.summary: Resale condo prices ticked back up in May 2026 after softening earlier in the year, even though the number of transactions eased. Sellers held firm on asking prices despite the quieter buying activity.
items.meaning: Buyers hoping for condo resale discounts should not count on them yet, as sellers are not under pressure to lower prices.

items.headline: June BTO round draws 22,312 applications, with one project the clear favourite
items.summary: More than 22,000 people applied for the June 2026 Build-to-Order exercise, showing that demand for new public flats remains strong. One project stood out with a disproportionately high number of applicants compared to the others in the same round.
items.meaning: Competition for popular BTO flats is still fierce, so it pays to have a backup project chosen before you ballot.

items.headline: Around 8,000 new HDB flats to launch in October 2026, with Bayshore and Toa Payoh projects tipped as hot picks
items.summary: HDB has confirmed it will offer roughly 8,000 flats in the October 2026 BTO exercise. Projects in Bayshore and Toa Payoh are expected to draw strong interest because of their locations near the coast and city fringe respectively.
items.meaning: If you are planning to ballot for a flat, mark October 2026 on your calendar and start shortlisting now, especially if you are open to Bayshore or Toa Payoh.

items.headline: Planned HDB site in Sembawang scaled back to preserve a patch of secondary forest
items.summary: Authorities have reduced the size of a new HDB development site in Sembawang after deciding to keep part of an existing secondary forest intact. The decision means fewer flats will be built there than originally planned.
items.meaning: Less supply coming to Sembawang than expected, which could firm up demand and prices for flats in that area.

items.headline: 32-year-old executive HDB flat in Kallang/Whampoa sells for a record $1.412 million
items.summary: A large, older executive flat in Kallang/Whampoa changed hands at $1.412 million, setting a new price record for its flat type in the area. Its size, central location, and remaining lease all played a role in the high price.
items.meaning: Big, centrally located older HDB flats can still fetch top dollar, which is a useful benchmark whether you are buying or thinking of selling one.

items.headline: 38-year-old HDB maisonette in Bukit Timah sells for a record $1.508 million
items.summary: A large, older maisonette-style HDB flat in Bukit Timah set a new price record for the town at $1,508,888. The sale reflects continued demand for spacious older flat types in well-established, prime HDB locations.
items.meaning: Older HDB flats in sought-after estates can still command very high prices, so do not write off your older flat as less valuable just because of its age.

items.headline: Several HDB projects hit their resale eligibility mark in 2026, opening up more supply
items.summary: A batch of HDB flats bought in the 2021 BTO wave are reaching the end of their five-year Minimum Occupation Period this year, meaning owners are now legally allowed to sell on the open market. This will add fresh resale inventory across multiple estates.
items.meaning: More resale HDB flats will come onto the market throughout 2026, giving buyers more choices but also adding competition for sellers in the same estates.

items.headline: Two new private housing sites released at Bright Hill and Farrer Park, one backed by years of unmet demand
items.summary: The government has put two new sites up for sale under the Government Land Sales programme, where developers bid to build private condos. The Farrer Park site in particular has had little new private housing supply for years, which analysts expect will attract strong bids from developers.
items.meaning: New private condos should eventually emerge at these locations, giving buyers in Bright Hill and Farrer Park more options where supply has been thin.
